metric.context: SME for Stratozone acquisition; product still in production
Google Cloud Value Engineering scaled from $150M baseline (10X), 9 months
Built decision support tool used by senior leadership for AI investment decisions
Synthesized 325 use cases into 22 patterns; 10 submitted for funding
Designed and led GCP Champs; 7X completion rate vs. baseline
Led Google Cloud Industry Business Architecture program
Converted Tribune relationship into Azure partnership
Every major program still running 3-7+ years post-departure: Stratozone→Migration Center, Industry BA→accreditation, VE→global structure

positioning.headline: Zero-to-One AI Program Builder: I architect programs for permanence

core-methodology.name: Permanence Architecture
core-methodology.description: Deliberate five-mechanism system ensuring programs survive organizational change and outlast the builder's tenure. Applied across 6 programs at 3 companies over 15+ years with 100% survival rate.

mechanisms.mechanism: Create standards for how work gets done, not just content—standards reduce cognitive load and can be enforced without original author
Define roles that make work someone's job—role definitions embed in HR systems and create accountability
Attach programs to persistent structures (accreditations, academies, products)—these have their own governance and funding
Scale participation to 100+ people who advocate for continuation—broad training creates diffuse ownership
Bring in external parties (consultants, partners, legal)—external credibility persists independent of internal sponsorship

differentiators.diff: Program permanence: 6 programs across 3 companies, all still running 3-7+ years after departure
Builder-architect hybrid: Designs strategy AND builds working tools
Zero-to-one builder: Stands up new functions from scratch
Rapid executive engagement: Workshop to CEO in weeks

Builder-not-operator: I create things that BECOME teams, then hire leaders—88 contributors via influence, ~100 Google interviews (people assessment), intentional pattern selecting OUT of time-consuming management incompatible with innovation work

technical-calibration.positioning: Applied AI strategist who can design and build lightweight implementations. NOT AI engineering leader. McDonald's agents are low-code (Copilot Studio, Power Automate, Rovo); Agentient work is more sophisticated (custom MCP servers, multi-agent orchestration in Python).

domain.name: Value Engineering
domain.honest-assessment: 1-2 years experience, few engagements, not passionate. Partnership role: identify opportunities for VE analysis, partner with Finance for detailed business cases. Finance depth insufficient for RevOps positioning.

position.title: Principal AI Architect (Generative AI)
position.reporting: Reports to Senior Director (Chief Architect) with dotted line to VP

activity.situation: McDonald's entered into a strategic partnership with Google Cloud; one of the key initiatives in this partnership was the deployment of McDonald's AI platform on Google Cloud that would support the AI agents and tools that McDonald's would build.
activity.task: Design and document target state architecture for enterprise AI platform on Google Cloud Platform covering all layers from user interface to governance.
activity.action: Designed comprehensive 10-layer architecture covering Interface, Agent Orchestration, Agent Runtime, Tools and Functions, Models, Knowledge and Data, MLOps/AIOps, Infrastructure, AI Developer Experience, and Governance. Defined three-platform model (Restaurant, Consumer, Company) with distinct requirements. Developed scoped architecture design focused on model routing, context management, and human-in-the-loop feedback. Made key architectural decisions including leveraging Vertex AI Agent Engine, Vertex AI RAG Engine, and Cloud Run for hosting AI agents and tools; developing agents using the Agent Development Kit; Confluent Kafka for event streaming; Gravitee for API integration; A2A protocol for integration with ServiceNow; and Model Context Protocol (MCP) for tools integration. Analyzed and constructed six classes of AI hosting configurations spanning Agent Engine, Cloud Run, Compute Engine, Kubernetes, and Google Distributed Cloud Edge. Compared and contrasted orchestration capabilities of ServiceNow and Google Cloud. Studied model cards to evaluate capabilities of ServiceNow's custom models versus Gemini models. Worked closely with legal counsel responsible for AI policies to address compliance requirements. Compiled strategy to incorporate compliance requirements into build-time through Cursor agents and skills. Created three agents to assess quality of architecture recommendations, generate architecture diagrams, and analyze trade-offs between solution alternatives. Collaborated with data team on strategy for constructing McDonald's knowledge graph and enabling semantic search and vector search.
activity.result: Designed 10-layer enterprise AI platform architecture endorsed by CIO as McDonald's strategic technical direction; implementation now underway. AI agents significantly accelerated creation of draft AI platform architecture blueprint—from zero to draft in four weeks, a process that would typically take months to complete.

activity.situation: A CEO mandate dictated the formulation of an AI strategy for McDonald's to respond to the competition, margin pressures, and a need to convey to investors what McDonald's strategy was for AI adoption.
activity.task: Create a business and technology AI strategy for McDonald's.
activity.action: Part of a small 4-person team of business and technology AI experts organizing and preparing 16 workshops (2 per function) over three months with eight different business functions. Researched and authored 10 AI megatrend papers using AI-assisted synthesis covering Global Impact, Global Tech, EDAA, Finance, IS&P, Legal, Supply Chain, and People functions, plus executive and technical summary reports; these were used as pre-reads for workshop participants to prepare them for ideation sessions. Collaborated with business stakeholders to construct workshop material guiding participants through ideation and prioritization workshops. Participated in workshops sharing ideas and insights on AI application in each function. Leveraged AI to extract results of brainstorming sessions from notes and recordings, then performed triage to combine similar ideas, documenting problem statements and solution hypotheses for 350 ideas. Analyzed all ideas to evaluate impact and effort to help sort and filter. Collaborated with technical peer to analyze ideas across all functions to identify common architecture patterns and opportunities to enable maximum use cases through shared development efforts. Deconstructed complex use cases into smaller components to aid buy vs. build decision-making. Collaborated with AI Engineering and Legal teams to ensure alignment. Applied process engineering techniques to analyze value streams and identify opportunities for applying AI to automate processes or improve decision making. In cases where baseline data was available, constructed simple business value models. To demonstrate the opportunity to construct valuable agents using low-code tools, developed a decision support tool for the Global Program Management Office providing senior leadership a tool to interrogate McDonald's central initiative tracking repository for funding decisions. Developed three additional specialized agents using Copilot Studio and Power Automate including a current state researcher for exhaustive Atlassian Confluence search and baseline reports, and a business architecture modeler for capability modeling.
activity.result: Comprehensive spreadsheet containing over 350 documented use cases for the adoption of AI agents at McDonald's. 22 architecture patterns identified, and commitment to proceed with 10 initial AI use cases obtained, with work underway to construct an engine for building and deploying AI agents at scale. Delivered 10 megatrend papers informing enterprise AI strategy.

activity.situation: Following company reorganization, McDonald's architecture function lacked standardized operating model. No consistent framework existed for architecture services, artifacts, or roles across Solution, Business, Enterprise, Data, and AI architecture domains.
activity.task: Establish comprehensive architecture operating model to standardize practices across the organization.
activity.action: Applied Six Sigma SIPOC methodology, SCAMPI/A assessment framework, BIZBOK, and TOGAF to develop comprehensive operating model. Defined 9 core services, standardized approximately 45 artifacts, documented 12 architecture services, and defined 14 architect roles. Mapped 40 value streams to architecture capabilities. Used AI to help refine architecture services and capture artifact definitions and best practices. Published all operating model documentation to Atlassian Confluence for global architectural organization access.
activity.result: Established architecture operating model covering 5 domains (Solution, Business, Enterprise, Data, AI). Created 9 core services, 45 standardized artifacts, 14 architect role definitions, and 12 documented architecture services. Mapped 40 value streams. Operating model adopted across architecture organization and published to Atlassian Confluence for global access.

activity.situation: McDonald's had a strategic goal to expand the 150 million strong loyalty memberships (My McDonald's Rewards).
activity.task: Develop a technology multi-year strategy for loyalty partnerships starting with a partnership with Amazon Prime.
activity.action: Attended workshops with multiple stakeholders in product and engineering to determine McDonald's consumer engagement strategy, which resulted in two key strategic initiatives: a loyalty linked partnership program with a strategic partner, and a subscription service. Reviewed the strategic roadmap to identify the technical requirements for each key stage in the loyalty linked partnership product roadmap. Analyzed impacted and newly created business capabilities. Analyzed customer journey maps and documented value streams for consumers, partners, and marketers. Performed a deep analysis with solution architects to identify and evaluate 7 solution alternatives for linking accounts. Documented tradeoffs and recommendations and produced flow diagrams and sequence diagrams. Collaborated closely with solution architects to construct the architecture blueprint for integration with Amazon Prime.
activity.result: Produced a 3-phase target state architecture starting with a point solution with one strategic partner, an expanded architecture for multi-partner support, and a marketplace for a scaled strategy paving way for McDonald's to significantly grow the customer loyalty program.

activity.situation: McDonald's is developing a subscription service to grow incremental system sales, boost frequency, and deepen brand loyalty, and drive sustained growth in integrated delivery.
activity.task: Assess subscription service feasibility and evaluate technical solution alternatives.
activity.action: Analyzed the impact and technical feasibility of 25 subscription constructs identified by McKinsey, and identified functional and non-functional requirements of the subscription service. Created and refined a business capability model to identify the business capabilities impacted by subscriptions, with emphasis on payment systems. Produced a comprehensive context diagram to map technology impact to MarTech stack including mParticle, Braze, SessionM, and Dynamic Yield. Constructed a vendor assessment framework to evaluate 5 subscription payment vendors and provided a technical recommendation.
activity.result: Delivered a report on the feasibility of various subscription constructs and a detailed vendor assessment with a recommended vendor to partner with.

activity.situation: McDonald's migrating enterprise systems to Google Cloud Platform but workforce lacked GCP skills. Need existed for structured training program to accelerate adoption and upskill employees.
activity.task: Design and lead cloud training program to accelerate GCP adoption and workforce upskilling.
activity.action: Designed and led comprehensive GCP Champs training program. Coordinated with Google Cloud for certification paths. Led program implementation including enrollment, coursework tracking, and certification preparation. Monitored participation and completion metrics.
activity.result: Designed and led GCP Champs training program: 154 direct participants, 100 certifications achieved, 7X course completion rate vs. baseline. Generated 2X pull-through effect with 166 additional employees participating. Participants completed 732 courses and passed 2,249 labs (6X increase). Program praised by CIO; being integrated into Global IT Tech Academy.

activity.situation: In early 2023, there was a massive gold rush for Generative AI, but sales teams were struggling to move past basic ChatGPT prompts. My partners and I identified a specific gap: a lack of specialized, high-quality sales enablement content that combined AI theory with practical sales execution.
activity.task: As the sole technical founder, my task was to build a production-ready SaaS platform from scratch in a highly volatile market. I needed to create an automated content engine that could produce expert-level material at a scale that a human team couldn't match.
activity.action: Architected and developed full-stack platform using React/Next.js frontend, Firebase backend (Functions, Auth, Storage, Firestore), Google Cloud Run for compute, and Stripe for payments. Built multi-agent content production system with 8 specialized AI agents using Claude and Gemini. Created 50+ sales best practice articles, 50+ podcasts, 50-60 AI prompt templates, and physical flashcard deck. Implemented hybrid parallel/sequential agent orchestration pattern.
activity.result: While the technical launch was a success—delivering a full-stack platform with 150+ assets and a functional payment gateway—the market response taught us a critical lesson in ICP (Ideal Customer Profile) alignment. We realized we built a 'vitamin' for individual contributors rather than a 'painkiller' for Sales Enablement leaders. I leveraged these architectural patterns and GTM insights to immediately pivot our strategy for my next venture, Agentient.

activity.situation: Pre-seed founders and aspiring entrepreneurs face a 90%+ failure rate, primarily due to weak product-market fit validation and underdeveloped go-to-market strategies—challenges amplified by limited resources and no access to experienced product advisors.
activity.task: Create open resources and AI-assisted tooling to help early-stage founders systematically validate product-market fit and execute effective launches, democratizing product development expertise typically available only to well-funded startups.
activity.action: Developing an AI-powered platform that guides founders through structured PMF assessment, research synthesis, and GTM planning. Built lightweight frameworks adapting enterprise product development methodologies for resource-constrained contexts. Applying modern AI stack (agentic workflows, multi-model orchestration) to reduce the expertise gap between first-time founders and seasoned product leaders.
activity.result: Platform in development; early users validating core PMF assessment workflows.

activity.situation: Research firms and early-stage startups require specialized expertise in enterprise AI, cloud strategy, and go-to-market execution that sits at the intersection of deep technical knowledge and business strategy.
activity.task: Provide expert guidance on AI/cloud architecture decisions, market dynamics, and organizational best practices to inform investment research and product strategy.
activity.action: Advised AlphaInsights on workflow automation, Tempus Research study on market share of LLM API providers and access channels, and Gerson Lehrman Group (GLG) on public cloud provider technology, organizational best practices in cloud computing, sales/GTM functions in cloud providers, and sales programs in innovative companies. Also served on the advisory board of MobiusEngine.ai, an executive coaching and job placement service.
activity.result: Completed 8 paid advisory engagements across 3 firms. Advisory inputs incorporated into published market research and product roadmaps.

position.title: Principal Program Manager
position.departure-context: Laid off in 2023 reduction; had back-to-back Exceeds Expectations ratings in prior two performance cycles (unusual at this level). Team was deprioritized, not performance.

activity.situation: Digital transformation increasingly owned by business leaders rather than IT. Google Cloud sales teams lacked industry knowledge to engage business executives. No unified methodology existed across previously siloed enablement teams.
activity.task: Build and manage largest cross-functional program of career to develop industry business architecture content enabling sales teams to engage business executives.
activity.action: Managed program with 88 contributors across 10 workstream teams and 3 cross-functional teams covering 12 industries. Engaged Accenture for 3-month consulting to develop initial bill-of-materials for 6 industries including trends, value chains, capability maps, metrics, maturity models, and data models. Created internal website to surface content. Launched at global sales conference and recruited volunteers. Partnered with Distinguished Engineer for executive sponsorship. Added McKinsey persona research and expanded to 12 industries. Developed unified engagement model based on design thinking. Launched 20% project to recruit Technical Program Managers.
activity.result: Delivered comprehensive business architecture content for 12 industries with 6 reference use cases. Led 88 contributors across 10 workstreams without direct authority—coordinated through granular task breakdown, two-week sprints, and peer recognition. Doubled traffic to industry enablement content. Program became basis for Principal Architect Level 300 accreditation standard—still in use 3+ years post-departure, demonstrating institutional anchoring.

activity.situation: SME resources for content production were scarce and content quickly became stale. Course production took 6-8 weeks requiring significant SME time. JWCC contract required 11 courses in 2 months over holiday period.
activity.task: Develop innovative content production pipeline to address SME scarcity and reduce course development time.
activity.action: Developed AI-powered content pipeline: source content transcription via Adobe Premiere speech-to-text, narrative synthesis via ChatGPT/Bard, manual accuracy editing, text-to-speech voiceover via WaveNet/WellSaid Labs, audio remastering via Adobe Audition, final video production via Adobe Premiere, modular course deployment to Intellum LMS. Applied pipeline to JWCC contract requirements and public sector enablement.
activity.result: Reduced course production time from 6-8 weeks to less than 1 week (85% reduction). Reduced SME time from days to hours (90% reduction). Successfully delivered 11 JWCC courses in 2 months. Put in charge of entire public sector enablement program. Content easily updated when products changed.

activity.situation: Enablement organization lacked SME resources for content production. No standardization existed in document artifacts and training content. Teams engaged reactively with last-minute requests creating inefficiencies and redundancies.
activity.task: Develop standardization framework for enablement content to improve efficiency and consistency.
activity.action: Developed 4-tier standards model reflecting level of enablement detail required. Created 5 standardized document templates and 6 detailed sales and technical course outlines. Built AppSheet tracking app to manage and track artifact status. Applied Bloom's taxonomy for learning objectives, sales cycle stage alignment, and learner retention assessment guidance.
activity.result: Standards adopted across enablement teams and integrated into GTM process for product launches. Shifted SME engagement from reactive to proactive. Reduced content pipeline inefficiencies. Improved upstream and downstream stakeholder satisfaction.
activity.permanence: Standards integrated into GTM process—still in use

activity.situation: Served as bridge role after Value Engineering transition. Sales teams needed competitive positioning against Azure and AWS. Leadership needed competitive analysis for executive communications.
activity.task: Research, analyze, and document competitive insights. Train Googlers and partners on competitive positioning.
activity.action: Authored AWS re:Invent 2019 synopsis for global sales force. Prepared Goldman Sachs survey response for CEO Thomas Kurian and senior leaders. Created and presented Azure compute training at global sales conference. Led and mentored UIUC postgraduate students on competitive analysis AI model using NER, relationship extraction, sentiment analysis, and summarization.
activity.result: Delivered AWS re:Invent analysis to global sales force. Contributed to CEO investor communications. Trained 500+ attendees on Azure compute competitive positioning with 4.6 average rating. Mentored university students on AI-powered competitive analysis.

activity.situation: Google Cloud Value Engineering was nascent function approximately 18 months old with approximately $150M in assessed business through Stratozone partnership. Function needed to scale to support Google Cloud growth. Multiple fragmented initiatives existed across organization including CloudPhysics and Velostrata without unified strategy.
activity.task: Develop, execute, and scale global value engineering strategy. Consolidate fragmented migration initiatives into cohesive program.
activity.action: Built comprehensive Global Assessment and Planning Program by consolidating Stratozone (TCO assessment), CloudPhysics (asset discovery), and Velostrata (VM migration) into end-to-end solution. Renegotiated Stratozone contract to secure additional capabilities at no cost by demonstrating 10X scale opportunity. Reinvested savings into global sales enablement tour covering 20 cities across Europe, North America, and Asia. Trained approximately one-third of global sales force on assessment and planning tools.
activity.result: Generated $1.5B in pipeline within 9 months representing 10X growth from $150M baseline. Improved forecast accuracy by 60%. Reduced sales cycle by 70%. Increased win rate for VM migration by 5%. Created end-to-end migration program from discovery to execution. Program structure adopted globally and continues 6+ years post-departure—constituency of ~1/3 of sales force trained across 20 cities created organizational persistence.

activity.situation: Google Cloud Engineering preferred acquiring discovery/assessment capabilities rather than building internally. Group Product Manager for Compute needed tooling to support VM migrations. Multiple acquisition candidates existed (CloudPhysics, Stratozone) requiring technical evaluation and strategic recommendation.
activity.task: Serve as subject matter expert for acquisition evaluation; provide recommendation on which company to acquire.
activity.action: Served as subject matter expert for Stratozone acquisition evaluation. Conducted build-vs-buy analysis and technical due diligence comparing CloudPhysics and Stratozone capabilities. Evaluated competing vendors and provided recommendation that informed acquisition decision—if recommendation had been negative, acquisition would not have proceeded. Participated in vendor negotiations alongside GPM. Maintained direct relationship with Stratozone CEO that facilitated deal progression.
activity.result: Served as SME for Google Cloud's Stratozone acquisition (closed 2020); provided recommendation that determined acquisition proceed—if negative, acquisition would not have happened. Product became Google Cloud Migration Center—still in production 7+ years later, demonstrating permanence architecture in action.

activity.situation: Strategic accounts needed value engineering support for cloud business cases. Deals required ROI justification and technical validation.
activity.task: Provide direct deal support for strategic accounts developing business cases for cloud adoption.
activity.action: Supported Walmart with business case for TPUs for real-time recommendations on Walmart.com. Worked with Pandora on data analysis initiative for song recommendation optimization. Assisted Broadcom with defect rate reduction analysis for microchip testing. Developed Equifax business value case for cloud security.
activity.result: Delivered business cases for strategic accounts including Walmart TPU recommendation engine, Pandora song recommendations, Broadcom defect analysis, and Equifax cloud security. Supported deal progression with quantified ROI analysis.

activity.situation: Google Cloud was nascent organization and new entrant in enterprise field. Primary challenge was establishing enterprise credibility with Fortune 500 customers accustomed to Microsoft and AWS. Enterprise security and compliance were key concerns blocking adoption.
activity.task: Support sales teams by conveying technical capabilities to C-level executives. Develop and deliver enterprise security and compliance messaging.
activity.action: Became de-facto security SME creating reusable security messaging for sales teams. Co-developed and presented workshop with product/engineering for hand-selected customer engineers globally establishing first security specialist team. Presented at RSA Conference, Advisen Risk Management Conference, and Rackspace conference. Supported key accounts including PayPal, Capital One, HSBC, Target, Best Buy, and Kohl's with security briefings.
activity.result: Established enterprise security credibility for Google Cloud in Central Region. Created first security specialist team through global customer engineer workshop. Developed reusable security messaging adopted by sales organization. Earned Google Cloud Architect certification. Published Fast Magazine article on AI, IoT, and productivity integration.

activity.situation: Highland Technologies (trading firm) processed algorithmic trades consuming approximately 120,000 cores on GCP. Failures appeared that were difficult to trace. Concurrent incident: SnapChat (major client) experienced outage. SREs discovered Cloud Front-end Service was being flooded by Highland's workload pattern.
activity.task: Investigate root cause of infrastructure constraints affecting two key customers and develop resolution strategy.
activity.action: Gained access to internal behind-the-scenes infrastructure tools through SREs. Discovered Cloud Front-end Service being flooded. Met with client to determine root cause: incredibly small job sizes each writing outputs to files read by next job creating massive I/O from thousands of simultaneous small file operations. Worked with SREs, product team, and engineering leadership. Collaborated with client to redistribute workloads and reduce I/O demands. Managed load while waiting for hardware expansion.
activity.result: Identified root cause as job design pattern flooding Cloud Front-end Service. Developed workload redistribution design with client. Short-term: managed load while waiting for hardware. Long-term: new hardware assembled and deployed. Unexpected win: redistributed design proved better than client's original architecture and was retained even after capacity available.

activity.situation: Kohl's was largest Central Region customer seeking to modernize technology stack including mobile backend, catalog search, and data analytics on Google Cloud.
activity.task: Coordinate Kohl's adoption of Google Cloud across multiple workstreams including Kubernetes, BigQuery, and DataProc.
activity.action: Coordinated Kubernetes adoption for mobile backend. Supported catalog search migration to GCP. Facilitated Hadoop migration to BigQuery and DataProc for analytics workloads.
activity.result: Supported Kohl's multi-workstream cloud adoption across mobile (Kubernetes), search (GCP), and analytics (BigQuery/DataProc). Established Google Cloud as strategic platform for Kohl's technology modernization.

activity.situation: Verizon Enterprise Solutions managing $800 million book of business seeking to expand cloud services portfolio. Executive sponsor who recruited team left company weeks after joining leaving organization without leadership or direction.
activity.task: Member of East Region Management Team focusing on cloud services business development, technology evangelism, customer engagement, and strategy enablement.
activity.action: Joined as Principal Client Partner on East Region Management Team with cloud services portfolio focus. Conducted business development activities for cloud services. Engaged customers on cloud strategy. After 6 months without strategy or clear responsibilities due to sponsor departure, began exploring other opportunities. Discovered Google Cloud role similar to Verizon and Microsoft experience.
activity.result: Brief tenure due to organizational instability when executive sponsor departed. Left after 6 months to join Google Cloud as Principal Strategic Advisor. Key lesson: executive sponsorship is critical for role viability in large organizations.

activity.situation: Gap existed in market for World of Tanks companion app on Windows platform. Microsoft ecosystem had engaged gaming community but no quality companion apps. Financial runway available from successful Microsoft years provided opportunity for entrepreneurship.
activity.task: Design, develop, and launch Windows companion application for World of Tanks gaming community.
activity.action: Managed 2-person development team in Ukraine with daily morning meetings. Handled product management, testing, and cloud services development. Designed application following Metro UI guidelines. Built cloud backend using Amazon Cognito for auth and Amazon RDS for data. Used Xamarin for cross-platform development with intent to port to iOS and Android. Launched within 3 months. Executed scrappy social media marketing campaign targeting gaming community.
activity.result: Launched in 3 months from start to first version. Achieved Top 10 ranking in lifestyle category in 4 countries. Reached Top 200 in major markets including US, UK, France, Germany, and Australia. Scaled back to single developer for maintenance in September 2015 when user engagement insufficient. Closed business December 2018. Key learnings: platform selection matters, engagement trumps vanity metrics, cash runway critical for consumer apps.

activity.situation: Microsoft investing heavily to secure marquee apps for Windows Store. Product groups had priority account lists and significant investment budgets. Role required structuring deals incorporating multiple Microsoft technologies.
activity.task: Secure strategic partnership deals with high-profile enterprise accounts through internal investment and cross-team collaboration.
activity.action: Built business cases for internal investment from Windows and Windows Phone product groups. Structured multi-technology deals incorporating Windows Phone, Windows 8, Xbox, Azure, and Advertising. Navigated internal funding processes with effectively 100% success rate for priority accounts. Applied "One Microsoft" approach working across Advertising, Devices, and Retail teams.
activity.result: Raised $2.5M of internal investment from various business groups and programs for strategic partnership deals in 2013-2014. Recognized as one of few "whale hunters" given largest brands to work with. Developed strong relationships with Corporate VP through multiple strategic meetings.

activity.situation: Predecessor unable to secure Walmart platform partnership in 3+ years. Walmart relationship strained after Windows 8 holiday sales disappointed. Microsoft Retail team under immense pressure for better next holiday season.
activity.task: Break through to secure Walmart platform partnership using beachhead strategy and cross-Microsoft collaboration.
activity.action: Targeted VUDU (Walmart's video streaming) as beachhead bringing Xbox app to market. Began Azure discussion for video processing. Applied "One Microsoft" approach coordinating Retail team (seeking holiday ideas), Advertising (seeking retail partnerships), and Xbox (VUDU relationship). Organized major meeting at Microsoft headquarters with Walmart Chief Merchandising Officer. Proposed Walmart app pre-installed on all Windows devices sold through Walmart. Shared Walgreens photo business win as proof point. Secured $500K funding for app development. Hired development partner to build Windows Phone and Windows 8 apps.
activity.result: Secured broader partnership beyond single app encompassing Xbox/VUDU, advertising exploration, and retail device strategy. Delivered Windows Phone and Windows 8 apps. In post-holiday debrief, Walmart leadership called out app workstream as highlight of Microsoft partnership for the year.

activity.situation: Scripps Networks owned valuable brands including HGTV, Food Network, DIY Network, and Travel Channel. Windows 8 launch approaching with need for major showcase applications. Brand managers had authority over app decisions.
activity.task: Secure Scripps Networks brands for Windows Phone, Windows 8, and Xbox platforms.
activity.action: Secured speaking slot at executive briefing. Met COO who arranged CEO meeting at CES Las Vegas. Secured CEO support for Food Network app on Windows/Windows Phone. Faced timeline pressure with months to deliver and insufficient developer resources. Personally coded significant portions of Windows 8 application in C# using WinRT APIs—learned new platform while delivering under deadline. Collaborated with technical evangelist colleague on architecture and remaining components.
activity.result: Food Network app became one of five major showcase applications for Windows 8 launch. Delivered under timeline pressure by personally coding significant portion of application. Demonstrated ability to combine business development with technical delivery.

activity.situation: Walgreens seeking to drive mobile photo printing service adoption. Partnership opportunity existed for Windows ecosystem integration.
activity.task: Structure Walgreens partnership for photo printing integration across Windows ecosystem.
activity.action: Developed partnership structure incorporating Windows Phone app with photo printing capability, API exposure for other developers to integrate, and OneDrive integration for direct photo printing from cloud storage.
activity.result: Delivered successful Walgreens partnership with Windows Phone app, developer API, and OneDrive integration. Used as proof point for subsequent Walmart discussions. Attempted to replicate model with Walmart Photo Labs.

activity.situation: AON CTO organization invited both Microsoft and Google to present on moving software to the cloud. Microsoft transitioning to cloud with "Software + Services" strategy and first major product BPOS (later Office 365).
activity.task: Win competitive engagement against Google by articulating Microsoft cloud strategy addressing enterprise security, confidentiality, and compliance concerns.
activity.action: Developed and presented Microsoft cloud migration strategy emphasizing gradual migration path while protecting security, confidentiality, and compliance. Positioned against Google's approach which failed to address enterprise concerns.
activity.result: Won competitive engagement against Google. AON became one of first major customers in Central Region to adopt BPOS. Invited to present at AON's global CTO conference in Rotterdam on cloud migration strategy. Asked by multiple Microsoft account teams to present on how to win BPOS deals against Google.

activity.situation: Tribune Media Company sales team struggling to negotiate contract renewal. EVP and CIO Steve Gable had "Content Cloud" initiative to centralize content across all media properties and reduce data center footprint.
activity.task: Strengthen Tribune partnership by identifying strategic technology opportunities beyond contract renewal.
activity.action: Hosted Silverlight workshop with key technical decision makers generating ideas for richer media experiences. Workshop findings forwarded to EVP Steve Gable who invited direct meeting. Learned about Content Cloud initiative. Received confidential disclosure permission to inform Tribune CTO about "Red Dog" (pre-release Azure). Secured pilot deploying Tribune content to pre-release Azure.
activity.result: Secured major Windows Azure deal with significant PR and marketing collateral. Delivered $1.5M annual operational cost reduction through accessible solution across media properties plus consolidated data centers. Created foundation for deeper Tribune relationship.

activity.situation: Tribune board asking about digital mobile strategy after iPad launch. Steve Gable (CIO promoted to EVP for Tribune Digital) sought mobile strategy guidance. Windows/Windows Phone had less than 1% market share requiring compelling case to bet on Windows.
activity.task: Develop comprehensive mobile strategy for Tribune properties to position Windows as strategic platform choice.
activity.action: Conducted deep industry research into advertising and distribution business. Gathered key insights and datapoints. Built lightweight prototype showing potential experience. Developed comprehensive vision encompassing all Tribune properties: newspapers, WGN-TV video/audio, LA Times movie/sports data, other Tribune businesses. Strategy so compelling that within one week Steve Gable introduced to Tribune CEO. Presented e-reader strategy to CEO who revealed planned tablet device development.
activity.result: Identified $3.3B tablet partnership opportunity through self-initiated market analysis; secured CEO presentation within weeks of initial engagement. Windows tablet deal lost due to platform issues outside control, but converted relationship into Windows Azure partnership with $1.5M annual cost savings. Recognition: Gold Club Nomination (2012), three consecutive Top Contributor Awards, MGX Best in Class Field Execution (2013).

activity.situation: Office 2007 and Windows Vista launching with need for enterprise showcase demonstrations. Major commodities exchange seeking to modernize investment modeling process.
activity.task: Promote and drive awareness and adoption of Microsoft new and emerging technologies through enterprise engagements.
activity.action: Integrated investment modeling process into Microsoft Office for major commodities exchange as Office 2007 launch showcase. Executed multi-million dollar Office business application deal with major companies. Supported major enterprise deals with technical evangelism for Office 2007 and Windows Vista launches.
activity.result: Delivered Office 2007 launch showcase integration for major commodities exchange. Executed multi-million dollar Office business application deal. Awarded Circle of Excellence 2008 (team award) for fiscal year performance.

activity.situation: Allstate Financial undergoing major application modernization. Three core back-office life systems needed consolidation into one. New system experienced major performance problems on critical path threatening project success. Engineer lacked experience with specific technology and IBM support not resolving issue.
activity.task: Lead one of largest programs in division consolidating life systems. Resolve critical performance issue blocking project success.
activity.action: Led team of application, infrastructure, and data architects deploying enterprise solutions. When performance crisis emerged, escalated to VP responsible for life and annuity systems. Further escalated to IBM management when standard support failed. IBM identified retired engineer in Colorado as expert in specific technology area. Hired expert as contractor for targeted engagement.
activity.result: Within two weeks of expert engagement, issue diagnosed and resolved. Project back on track. Successfully consolidated three life systems into one. Key lesson: when standard support fails, escalate to executive level and find domain experts even through unconventional means.

activity.situation: Architecture review board decision-making was slow and lacked transparency. Lack of reuse of existing service-oriented architectures. Criteria for vendor selection ambiguous and left to tenured architects who often lacked integration knowledge. Decisions delayed or rejected without clear justification.
activity.task: Complete Six Sigma Black Belt project improving enterprise architecture decision-making processes.
activity.action: Reviewed data on review board decisions and outcomes. Reviewed API usage data. Interviewed stakeholders throughout organization. Developed scoring mechanism with assessment criteria across multiple EA views: data, application, development, operations, network, security, integration, and compliance. Created fast-track threshold for solutions scoring above threshold. Developed SOA metrics tracking both development and consumption of reusable assets.
activity.result: Accelerated decision-making with more robust criteria. Replaced ambiguous interpretations with clear, understood criteria. Created metrics for tracking SOA initiative performance. Contributed to Six Sigma Black Belt certification (October 2005).

activity.situation: Policy processing SLA was 28 days but actual time was 35 days. Longer processing led to higher rejection rates. Revenue impact significant especially for policies over $500K. Each department had SLA as range with 20% variance leading departments to operate at upper end while individual scorecards showed green but aggregate exceeded threshold.
activity.task: Complete COO-sponsored Six Sigma Black Belt project reducing policy cycle time.
activity.action: Analyzed policy cycle time data including duration by department, face value, and demographics. Conducted value stream analysis of new business process. Reviewed input/output metrics, issues, roles, and systems at each stage. Identified root cause: SLA structure with ranges allowed departments to operate at upper bounds creating aggregate overrun. Presented findings to COO. Identified low-hanging fruit to reduce cycle time in each department to average. COO instructed department leaders to implement changes. Implemented control plan to monitor improvements.
activity.result: Reduced policy cycle time from 35 days to 28 days (20% improvement). Improved policy acceptance rate by 14%. Completed Six Sigma Black Belt certification (October 2005).

activity.situation: Assigned to lead front-end development team for large B2B application at Manpower. Complex project with pressing deadlines. Team member's code causing performance problems but resistant to feedback blaming architecture.
activity.task: Lead front-end development team and resolve critical performance issue threatening project delivery.
activity.action: Developed prototype demonstrating performance improvement approach. When colleague unconvinced, raised impasse in daily team meeting. Project manager reviewed code and saw merit. Rewrote problematic code in few hours. Applied conflict resolution skills: let other party speak without interruption, identify exact causes, ask leading questions, focus on problem not person.
activity.result: Achieved 5X performance improvement. Reduced code from 3,000 lines to 100 lines (97% reduction). Improved maintainability and extensibility. Successfully delivered complex B2B application under deadline pressure.

activity.situation: After successful Manpower project, opportunity to lead Microsoft Technologies practice. Consulting market facing pressure from offshore competition during dot-com aftermath.
activity.task: Lead Microsoft Technologies practice managing consultants and driving business development.
activity.action: Managed team of 10 consultants. Led pre-sales activities including positioning, selling services, and proposals. Developed service strategy. Won notable deal with Audio Visual Services Corporation (AVSC) for mobile Pocket PC application tracking audiovisual equipment valued at $1M for 3 resources over 6 months. Established firm as premium mid-market consulting partner in Midwest.
activity.result: Led 10-person consulting team. Achieved approximately 50% win rate on proposals. Closed $1M AVSC deal. Left when dot-com bust and offshore competition shifted market to cost focus making premium positioning difficult.

activity.situation: Strong Funds (investment firm) had legacy CRM system used by investment advisors with Y2K issues and lacking required features.
activity.task: Lead development of new CRM application addressing Y2K compliance and feature requirements.
activity.action: Led small development team. Captured requirements from investment advisors. Architected new application in Visual Basic .NET. Structured code base, designed user interface, developed subsystems, tested application, and co-authored documentation.
activity.result: Delivered new CRM system that better met advisor needs with improved performance and Y2K compliance.

activity.situation: Strong Funds had no central place to manage login credentials across systems. Help desk overloaded with credential issues. Security risk existed as credentials not removed when employees left.
activity.task: Develop centralized identity management system for credential management across platforms.
activity.action: Gathered requirements from stakeholders. Identified key systems requiring management. Studied API documentation for third-party platforms. Developed and tested identity management code in Visual Basic .NET.
activity.result: Delivered identity management system managing credentials across Microsoft Windows, Novell, various Unix systems, and internal applications. Reduced help desk load and improved security posture.

activity.situation: SC Johnson had no common authentication/authorization mechanism for custom applications creating security risk and redundant disparate systems.
activity.task: Co-architect authentication and authorization framework for use across SC Johnson custom applications.
activity.action: Co-architected framework design. Developed authentication layer. Co-designed authorization layer and encryption subsystem. Performed testing and documentation.
activity.result: Delivered single, highly performant auth framework used across SC Johnson custom applications. Eliminated security gaps and redundant authentication systems.

activity.situation: Infrastructure deployment projects at Metropolitan Life and Engen Petroleum involved deploying thousands of PCs through very time-consuming manual process.
activity.task: Automate software deployment process to improve deployment efficiency.
activity.action: Leveraged software development background to develop scripting languages for automating software deployment on PCs. Given dedicated office with direction to automate as much deployment as possible.
activity.result: Achieved 5X faster deployment enabling 20 machines per person per day compared to previous highly manual process. Automation became company differentiator. Earned MCSE certification (1995).

position.title: Trainee Mainframe Operator / System Software Programmer

activity.situation: Company expanding team to manage non-mainframe systems as part of distributed computing strategy adopting PCs. Needed programmers for driver development.
activity.task: Develop and maintain drivers for mid-range machine to mainframe communication.
activity.action: Invested in personal PC to learn installation and operation. Completed company-provided COBOL programming training and certification. Applied for and received position as System Software Programmer. Developed and maintained printer drivers and scanner drivers for mid-range to mainframe communication. Completed Diploma in Datametrics (Computer Science) at University of South Africa.
activity.result: Transitioned from mainframe operations to system software development. Delivered printer and scanner drivers for mid-range machine to mainframe communication. Earned COBOL certification and completed computer science diploma. Foundation for transition to PC ecosystem consulting.

degree.name: Bachelor of Commerce (BComm)
degree.major: General Management (Finance, Marketing, Operations) + IT Management
degree.notes: Completed while working full-time through distance learning

degree.name: Diploma in Datametrics
degree.notes: Completed while working full-time through distance learning

certification.name: Six Sigma Black Belt
certification.projects: EA decision-making improvement, COO-sponsored policy cycle time reduction

award.name: Circle of Excellence Award
award.context: Team award for fiscal year performance

award.name: Circle of Excellence Award
award.context: Team award for fiscal year performance

award.name: Special Agent Award
award.context: Cloud security presentations to Electronic Crimes Task Force

award.name: Contribution Award
award.context: Cloud security education for Electronic Crimes Task Force

award.name: CIO Recognition
award.context: GCP Champs program praised by CIO

activity.organization: US Secret Service Electronic Crimes Task Force (ECTF)
activity.description: Delivered cloud security presentations to private sector, academic sector, Secret Service agents, and DEA analysts

activity.organization: Chicago Museums (Field Museum, Museum of Science and Industry, Chicago Children's Museum, Illinois Holocaust Museum)
activity.description: Microsoft Kinect and Photosynth integration into digital exhibits
